summary: O'Neil shares her preparation for a Slate Money podcast episode, admitting uncertainty about why patent trolls deserve their negative reputation. She tentatively defends the patent system as working well when time limitations aren't extended and patents are valid, while inviting readers to challenge her view. The post also teases other podcast topics and a mysterious 'number of the week.'
key_points: Patent trolls may not be as problematic as their reputation suggests
The patent system generally works well when time limits are respected and patents are legitimate
Open invitation for readers to provide counterarguments
money_quotes: To tell you the truth, so far I don't know why patent trolls are all that bad, besides the fact that they obviously have a terrible sounding name.
It seems like the patent system works in many ways for good, at least when there's no weird extensions of the time limitations and the original patent was valid.
key_insight: The negative framing of 'patent trolls' may obscure a more nuanced reality about how the patent system functions.
